summary: Files that export `async function` Server Actions must start with the `'use server'` directive on the first program statement. Without the directive, Next.js compiles the function as a regular module export, exposing server code to client bundles or breaking the action invocation contract. This recipe maps to plugin rule `rules-as-tests/require-use-server-directive` (R20).
bestPractices: Place `'use server';` as the very first statement of any file exporting `async function` actions.
Use a single double-quoted `"use server";` form per file — do not mix with `'use client'`.
Co-locate Server Actions in `app/**/actions.ts` files for visibility.
antiPatterns: Exporting `async function` from a file with no directive (Next 15 silently ships server code to the client).
Writing `'use server'` after an import or a side-effect statement (must be first).
Using `'use server'` and `'use client'` in the same file (mutually exclusive).
